summary: Boombey, distant audience recording but a fiery ’78 show highlighted by an electrifying Black Peter, Friend of the Devil and Stagger Lee.
review: The performance shines despite a muffled, back‑of‑the‑room sound that many reviewers describe as fuzzy, booming or clouded. Jerry’s playing is often praised – especially his vocal fire on Black Peter and his energy on the first set – while the band delivers strong jams of Stagger Lee, Playin' in the Band and other classics. The audience‑sourced tape suffers from cuts, splices and a lack of clarity, but fans who can “get used to it” still find the show one of the better ’78 outings.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers love the musical performance (especially Jerry and key songs) but uniformly note poor, distant audience recording quality; the weighting yields a solid above‑average overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Fuzzy, back‑of‑the‑room distant sound; boomy and cloudy with occasional cuts/splices. EQ helps but the tape lacks clarity and detail.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Energetic first set with Jerry on fire; solid second‑set jams despite recording issues, overall pacing keeps the show engaging.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Mixed notes – occasional vocal flubs early, but generally on fire with powerful guitar and a standout Black Peter performance.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass was so strong it threatened to blow speakers; his playing felt forceful.
